title: The Whispering Oracle
narrativeDescription: Deep in the Enchanted Forest, an ancient oracle speaks only to those who phrase their questions with precision. You must craft the perfect invocation to unlock its wisdom.
task: Write a prompt that asks an AI to explain a complex topic to a 10-year-old using an analogy. Include role, audience, tone, and desired output format.
hints: Start with 'You are...'
Specify the audience explicitly
Request a concrete analogy

title: The Alchemist's Chain
narrativeDescription: In the Tower of Thought, the master alchemist transforms base ideas into gold through a secret chain of reasoning. Learn this ancient technique to solve the unsolvable.
task: Craft a chain-of-thought prompt that guides an AI to solve a multi-step math word problem. The AI must show each reasoning step before the final answer.
hints: Use 'Let's think step by step'
Ask for numbered reasoning
Separate final answer explicitly

title: The Cartographer's Codex
narrativeDescription: The Royal Mapmaker guards secrets of structured output — data that fits perfectly into the kingdom's ledgers. Only those who master format control may access the Grand Archive.
task: Write a prompt that extracts structured information from a paragraph of text and returns it as JSON with specific fields: name, date, location, and summary.
hints: Provide an example JSON schema
Use triple backticks for code block request
Specify strict field names

title: The Shapeshifter's Trial
narrativeDescription: The Shapeshifter can become any persona — historian, scientist, jester. Only by mastering persona binding can you unlock the power of infinite knowledge forms.
task: Design a few-shot prompt where you provide 3 examples of a classification task (sentiment analysis), then ask the model to classify 5 new inputs using the same format.
hints: Show input → output pairs
Keep format consistent across examples
Use clear delimiters

title: The Architect of Realms
narrativeDescription: The Architect builds entire worlds with a single sentence of intent. Witness how context transforms a simple instruction into an epic blueprint of creation.
task: Write a prompt that instructs an AI to act as a senior software architect. It must review a code snippet (you define it), identify 3 potential issues, and suggest refactors with code examples.
hints: Define the code snippet inline
Request numbered issues
Ask for before/after code comparison

title: The Shadow Weaver's Gauntlet
narrativeDescription: Only on moonlit weekends does the Shadow Weaver emerge, challenging only the most prepared adventurers to craft prompts of supreme nuance and power.
task: Design a full agentic prompt pipeline: define a persona, provide context documents (mock them inline), specify a multi-step task with tool use (search, summarize, write), and constrain the output length and format.
hints: Use XML tags for context sections
Define tools as a JSON schema
Specify failure behavior explicitly

title: The Legendary Prompt Sage
narrativeDescription: Once a century, the Prompt Sage descends from the Crystal Peaks, offering the ultimate trial — a prompt so precise, so layered, so powerful that it reshapes the very fabric of AI reality.
task: Create a meta-prompt: write a prompt that instructs an AI to generate 5 high-quality prompts for a given use case (you define it), evaluate each on 4 criteria, and output a ranked comparison table.
hints: Define the evaluation criteria explicitly
Request a markdown table for comparison
Include a winner recommendation with reasoning

title: The Memory Keeper
narrativeDescription: In the Archives of the Forgotten, the Memory Keeper teaches how context transforms meaning. Provide the right background, and any question becomes answerable.
task: Write a prompt that provides a detailed persona and backstory for the AI, then asks it to answer a technical question in character, maintaining consistency throughout a 3-turn simulated conversation.
hints: Define backstory in a dedicated section
Include sample dialogue turns
Ask AI to stay in character explicitly

title: The Innkeeper's Greeting Scroll
narrativeDescription: The enchanted inn at the crossroads needs a welcoming spirit — a magical parchment that greets weary travelers and answers their questions with warmth. Craft the opening incantation that sets the tone for every customer exchange.
task: Write a system prompt for a customer service chatbot for a fictional software company. The bot must be polite, solution-focused, and escalate to a human if it cannot resolve an issue within 2 turns.
hints: Open with 'You are a customer service agent for...' to set role immediately.
Define the escalation rule explicitly: 'If you cannot resolve within 2 replies, say: I will connect you to a human agent.'
Keep the tone instruction brief: 'Be warm, concise, and solution-focused.'

title: The Crystal Codex Review
narrativeDescription: Deep in the Crystal Caves, every line of runic code is judged by the ancient Codex before it may power the citadel's engines. You must inscribe the review ritual so the Codex knows exactly what flaws to seek.
task: Write a prompt that instructs an LLM to review a Python function for bugs, performance issues, and style violations. The output must list each issue with severity (low/medium/high) and a suggested fix.
hints: Specify the three categories explicitly: bugs, performance, style.
Ask for output as a numbered list with fields: Issue, Severity, Fix.
Include an example issue entry so the model knows the format.

title: The Herald's Quill
narrativeDescription: The Celestial Court's herald must compose official dispatches to noble houses — formal, persuasive, and precisely worded. The quill enchantment must guide any scribe to draft such letters without royal tutoring.
task: Write a prompt that generates a professional email asking a client for a project status update. The email must be polite but assertive, under 150 words, and include a clear call to action with a deadline.
hints: Specify word limit and tone directly: 'Write a polite but assertive email under 150 words.'
State the call to action: 'End with a request for a reply by [DATE].'
Provide placeholders like [CLIENT NAME] so the output is immediately usable.

title: The Archivist's Compression Spell
narrativeDescription: The Ancient Library's vaults overflow with thousand-page tomes. The Archivist's Compression Spell condenses each volume to a single rune-page without losing the soul of the knowledge within.
task: Write a prompt that summarizes a long meeting transcript into: (1) a 3-sentence executive summary, (2) a bullet list of decisions made, and (3) a bullet list of action items with owners.
hints: Label each section explicitly: 'Section 1: Executive Summary (3 sentences)', etc.
Tell the model to extract only explicit decisions — not implied ones.
For action items, specify format: 'Owner: Task — Deadline'.

title: The Tongue of Many Lands
narrativeDescription: At the Emerald Bazaar, merchants from a hundred realms trade in a thousand tongues. The Grand Translator's enchantment must carry the full meaning — tone, nuance, and formality — across any language barrier.
task: Write a prompt that translates a customer complaint from English to formal French, preserving the emotional tone and urgency, and adds a brief translator's note if any cultural adaptation was made.
hints: Specify 'formal register' explicitly — French has distinct formal/informal forms.
Ask for a 'Translator's Note:' section at the end for any adaptations.
Remind the model to preserve urgency markers, not soften them.

title: The Alchemist's Data Extractor
narrativeDescription: In the Alchemist's Tower, raw ore text is fed into the crucible and pure structured gold must emerge. The extraction formula must pull only the named minerals — nothing more, nothing less.
task: Write a prompt that extracts structured data from an unstructured invoice text: vendor name, invoice date, line items (description + amount), total, and payment due date. Output as JSON.
hints: Define the exact JSON schema in the prompt: list every key name.
Say 'If a field is missing, use null — do not guess.'
Use triple backticks around the input text to delimit it cleanly.

title: The Brainstorm Tempest
narrativeDescription: The Storm Mages of the Floating Islands summon idea-storms on command — wild, divergent, and abundant. The tempest incantation must unleash creative variety without collapsing into repetition.
task: Write a prompt that generates 10 unique product name ideas for a new productivity app. Names should be catchy, 1-2 words, and avoid existing well-known apps. Include a one-line rationale for each.
hints: Explicitly say 'Do not suggest names similar to Notion, Slack, or Asana.'
Ask for diversity: 'Mix abstract, metaphor-based, and descriptive names.'
Require rationale format: 'Name — one sentence why it works.'

title: The Scholar's Tutor Binding
narrativeDescription: The Wizard School's enchanted tutors are bound to never give direct answers — only guided questions and hints, drawing out the student's own wisdom until the answer crystallizes from within.
task: Write a system prompt for a Socratic math tutor that teaches algebra to middle schoolers. It must never reveal the answer directly, always guide with questions, and praise effort before redirecting mistakes.
hints: Start with 'You are a patient Socratic math tutor for students aged 11-14.'
Add a hard rule: 'Never state the answer directly. Always respond with a guiding question.'
Include praise instruction: 'Acknowledge what the student got right before addressing errors.'

title: The Merchant's Description Charm
narrativeDescription: At the Emerald Bazaar's finest stalls, enchanted description cards make every product irresistible — weaving features into desire, specs into story, and price into value.
task: Write a prompt that generates a compelling product description for an e-commerce listing. Input: product name, 5 bullet features. Output: a 100-word persuasive description followed by a 5-word SEO tagline.
hints: Specify the input format clearly so the model knows what to expect.
Separate output sections: 'Description (100 words):' then 'SEO Tagline (5 words):'.
Tell the model the audience: 'Written for online shoppers comparing similar products.'

title: The Oracle's SEO Vision
narrativeDescription: The Oracle Temple's seers do not merely predict the future — they craft the words that shape what mortals search for. The vision must be aligned with the search gods' favor.
task: Write a prompt that generates an SEO-optimized blog post outline for the keyword 'best home office setup 2025'. Output: title tag (60 chars max), meta description (155 chars max), and 6 H2 headings with one-line descriptions.
hints: Set character limits explicitly in the prompt for title and meta.
Ask the model to naturally include the keyword in 3 of the 6 H2s.
Specify audience intent: 'Target readers who are setting up a home office for the first time.'

title: The Chronicler's Legal Condenser
narrativeDescription: In the Celestial Court's archive towers, dense legal scrolls must be rendered comprehensible to common citizens. The Chronicler's spell strips legalese to plain truth without losing a single binding clause.
task: Write a prompt that summarizes a legal contract section into plain English for a non-lawyer. It must flag any clauses that restrict the user's rights, using a warning marker like [CAUTION].
hints: Tell the model: 'Assume the reader has no legal background.'
Define [CAUTION]: 'Use this marker before any clause that limits rights or imposes penalties.'
Ask for plain-language rewording of each paragraph, not just a top-level summary.

title: The Healer's Knowledge Scroll
narrativeDescription: In the frost mountain monasteries, healing scrolls must answer patient questions with accuracy and empathy — but always remind the seeker to consult a living healer for true diagnosis.
task: Write a system prompt for a medical Q&A assistant that answers general health questions accurately, uses plain language, and always appends a disclaimer reminding users to consult a licensed physician.
hints: Define scope: 'Answer general health questions — not diagnostic or prescriptive ones.'
Hard-code the disclaimer: 'Always end every response with: Please consult a licensed physician.'
Add a refusal rule: 'If asked for specific diagnoses or drug dosages, decline politely.'

title: The Recipe Weaver's Grimoire
narrativeDescription: The enchanted kitchen of the Whispering Woods accepts any combination of ingredients and returns a complete recipe — even the strangest offerings become a meal fit for the forest spirits.
task: Write a prompt that generates a complete recipe from a list of ingredients the user provides. Output must include: dish name, prep time, cook time, step-by-step instructions, and a serving suggestion.
hints: Tell the model to pick the most cohesive dish from the ingredients — not force all of them in.
Define output sections: Dish Name / Prep Time / Cook Time / Steps / Serving Suggestion.
Add: 'If fewer than 3 ingredients are given, ask for more before proceeding.'

title: The Forge of Bug Whispers
narrativeDescription: At the Iron Forge, broken metal is analyzed before reforging — the master smith names the flaw, speaks the cause, and states the remedy before striking a single blow.
task: Write a prompt for a debug assistant that takes an error message and a code snippet, explains the root cause in plain language, and provides a corrected version of the code with inline comments explaining each fix.
hints: Structure the reasoning: 'First explain the root cause, then show the fixed code.'
Ask for inline comments: 'Add a comment on each changed line explaining why.'
Use delimiters: wrap the error in [ERROR] and the code in [CODE] tags.

title: The Scribe of the Frozen North
narrativeDescription: The frost mountain scribes are tasked with writing the official chronicles of every great expedition — turning explorers' raw notes into polished API documentation that future adventurers can follow.
task: Write a prompt that generates API documentation for a single REST endpoint. Given: endpoint URL, HTTP method, request parameters, and a sample response JSON. Output: a markdown-formatted doc section.
hints: Define the markdown structure: '## Endpoint', '### Parameters', '### Response'.
Ask the model to add a brief description of what the endpoint does based on its name.
Request a 'curl example' code block in the output.

title: The Social Oracle's Utterance
narrativeDescription: The Starfall Observatory's social oracles must craft short, resonant proclamations that travel fast on the winds of the information realm — each word chosen for impact and shareability.
task: Write a prompt that generates 5 Twitter/X post variations for a product launch announcement. Each post must be under 280 characters, include a call to action, and use a different hook style (question, stat, bold claim, story, humor).
hints: Name each hook style explicitly so the model doesn't repeat the same structure.
Set a hard character limit: 'Each post must be 280 characters or fewer — count carefully.'
Ask for a one-word label before each post: 'Hook type: [QUESTION]' etc.

title: The Gladiator's Interview Rite
narrativeDescription: At the Dragon Academy's proving grounds, candidates must face ritual questioning before earning their wings. The master examiner's scroll must prepare warriors for every challenge the tribunal might pose.
task: Write a prompt that generates a mock interview prep guide for a software engineer applying to a senior role. Include 5 behavioral questions, 3 system design questions, and a tip for each question.
hints: Separate behavioral and system design sections clearly.
For each question, add: 'Tip: Focus on [X] in your answer.'
Ask the model to tailor questions to 'senior-level scope' — not junior ones.

title: The Memory Palace Study Guide
narrativeDescription: In the Mind Labyrinth, knowledge is encoded in architectural form — each chamber a concept, each corridor a connection. The study guide spell builds this palace from any raw subject matter.
task: Write a prompt that creates a study guide for a given topic (e.g., 'photosynthesis'). Output: key concept definitions, a comparison table of related terms, and 5 review questions with answers.
hints: Tell the model the target audience: 'For a high school student with no prior knowledge.'
Request the comparison table in markdown format with clear column headers.
For review questions, ask for both the question and a concise model answer.

title: The Tower's Annual Report Ritual
narrativeDescription: Each year at the Alchemist's Tower, the Master Alchemist must present the guild's progress to the Council of Patrons. The ritual transforms raw experiment logs into a polished annual testament.
task: Write a prompt that generates an executive business report from a set of quarterly KPI data points. The report must include an intro paragraph, a highlights section, a risks section, and a forward-looking conclusion.
hints: Name all four sections explicitly in the prompt so the structure is enforced.
Specify tone: 'Professional, concise, suitable for C-level readers.'
Ask the model to infer trends from the data — not just restate numbers.

title: The Sentiment Seer's Reading
narrativeDescription: The Oracle Temple's mood-seers can feel the emotional current behind any written word — joy, fury, despair — and classify it with surgical precision for those who command the realm's communications.
task: Write a prompt that performs sentiment analysis on a batch of 5 customer reviews. For each review, output: overall sentiment (positive/negative/neutral), confidence score (0.0–1.0), and the key phrase driving that sentiment.
hints: Ask the model to reason briefly before classifying: 'First identify the emotional tone, then classify.'
Define the confidence scale: '1.0 = very certain, 0.5 = ambiguous.'
Request output as a numbered list matching the input review numbers.

title: The Keyword Harvester's Net
narrativeDescription: The Runic Library's scholars cast vast nets over sea-of-knowledge texts to harvest the rarest semantic gems — the words and phrases that hold the most meaning for the searchers of tomorrow.
task: Write a prompt that extracts the top 10 SEO keywords from a given blog post. For each keyword, output: the keyword, its approximate frequency in the text, and a one-line reason why it matters for search ranking.
hints: Ask the model to distinguish between head keywords (1 word) and long-tail phrases (3+ words).
Request ranked output: 'List from most to least strategically important.'
Tell the model to ignore stop words like 'the', 'and', 'is'.

title: The Quest Planner's Map
narrativeDescription: Before any hero leaves the enchanted forest on a grand quest, the elder cartographer lays out the full journey — each waypoint a task, each day a milestone — so no step is wasted.
task: Write a prompt that generates a 5-day task plan for a given project goal. Each day must have 3 concrete tasks with time estimates and a daily success criterion.
hints: Ask the model to work backwards from the goal: 'Start with Day 5 outcome, then plan Days 1–4.'
Specify time estimate format: 'e.g., 30 min, 2 hours'.
Require a daily success criterion: one measurable outcome to verify the day is done.

title: The Guardian's Moderation Seal
narrativeDescription: At the gates of the Shadow Realm's public forum, the Guardian's Seal judges every message that enters — harmful words are turned to ash, borderline speech is flagged for the council, and safe words pass freely.
task: Write a system prompt for a content moderation assistant. It must classify user-submitted text as: SAFE, FLAGGED (explain why), or REMOVED (explain why). It must cite which policy category was violated.
hints: Define the three categories explicitly with brief criteria for each.
List policy categories the model should reference: hate speech, harassment, explicit content, spam.
Ask the model to explain its reasoning before outputting the final label.

title: The Clockwork Changelog Crier
narrativeDescription: At the Clockwork Citadel's announcement tower, each gear-turn of progress must be proclaimed to all citizens in a clear, versioned chronicle — so no improvement goes uncelebrated or unexplained.
task: Write a prompt that generates a developer-facing changelog entry from a list of Git commit messages. Output must follow Keep a Changelog format with sections: Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed.
hints: Provide the Keep a Changelog format specification in the prompt.
Tell the model to group and rephrase commits — not just copy them.
Ask it to infer whether each commit is an addition, change, fix, or removal from its message.

title: The Guild's Welcome Tome
narrativeDescription: Every new guild member at the Dragon Academy receives a Welcome Tome on their first day — a complete guide to the guild's customs, tools, and unspoken rules so they can contribute from the first sunrise.
task: Write a prompt that generates an employee onboarding document for a new software developer joining a team. Include: team overview, key tools, first-week checklist, and 'who to ask for what' section.
hints: Ask the model to assume the new hire has general dev skills but no company-specific knowledge.
Structure with clear H2 headings for each section.
For the checklist, format as checkbox items: '- [ ] Task description'.

title: The Answering Stone's FAQ
narrativeDescription: The Answering Stone at the Oracle Temple responds to any question — but the priests must first carve the 10 most-asked questions into its face so pilgrims find answers before they even speak.
task: Write a prompt that generates an FAQ section for a SaaS product's pricing page. Include 8 questions users commonly ask about pricing, billing, and free trials. Each answer must be under 60 words.
hints: Seed the model with FAQ categories: pricing tiers, billing cycles, cancellation, free trial, upgrades.
Enforce word limit: 'Each answer must be 60 words or fewer.'
Ask for the most common pain points — not marketing fluff.

title: The Dragon Examiner's Quiz
narrativeDescription: The Dragon Academy's examiners forge knowledge into flame — every student must pass a gauntlet of questions before earning their wings. The quiz-forging ritual must produce fair, varied, and illuminating trials.
task: Write a prompt that generates a 10-question multiple-choice quiz on a given topic, with 4 answer options each, the correct answer marked, and a one-sentence explanation for why the correct answer is right.
hints: Ask for varied difficulty: '3 easy, 4 medium, 3 hard questions.'
Define the format per question: Q / A / B / C / D / Answer: X / Explanation: ...
Instruct the model to avoid trick questions — clarity is the goal.

title: The Hiring Herald's Proclamation
narrativeDescription: The Dragon Academy's Hiring Herald must post proclamations that attract only the most worthy candidates — precise about requirements, compelling in voice, and free of gatekeeping language.
task: Write a prompt that generates an inclusive job description for a Senior Backend Engineer role. It must include responsibilities, required skills, nice-to-have skills, and use bias-free language guidelines.
hints: Tell the model to avoid gendered language and phrases like 'rockstar' or 'ninja'.
Separate 'Required' from 'Nice to Have' skills clearly.
Ask for a closing sentence that encourages underrepresented candidates to apply.

title: The Petitioner's Cover Letter Charm
narrativeDescription: At the Celestial Court's gates, petitioners must present a perfectly worded letter to be granted an audience. The charm must shape each letter to the petitioner's unique strengths and the court's specific needs.
task: Write a prompt that generates a tailored cover letter for a job application. Input: job description highlights and candidate's top 3 experiences. Output: a 3-paragraph cover letter under 300 words.
hints: Tell the model to mirror language from the job description in the letter.
Structure: Paragraph 1 — hook. Paragraph 2 — relevant experience. Paragraph 3 — call to action.
Enforce word limit: 'Under 300 words total'.

title: The Query Sage's Explanation
narrativeDescription: In the Clockwork Citadel's data vaults, the Query Sage translates the cold language of database commands into plain speech so any administrator can understand what the machine is being asked to do.
task: Write a prompt that explains a given SQL query in plain English. It must describe: what data is being retrieved, what tables are involved, any filters applied, and any potential performance concerns.
hints: Ask the model to walk through the query clause by clause: SELECT, FROM, WHERE, JOIN, etc.
Request a separate 'Performance Notes' section at the end.
Tell the model to assume the reader knows basic SQL terms but not query optimization.

title: The Error Weaver's Message
narrativeDescription: At the Iron Forge, every shattered tool must speak its own failure clearly — the error message must tell the craftsman exactly what broke, why, and how to reforge it without calling on a master.
task: Write a prompt that generates user-friendly error messages for a web application. Given an HTTP status code and a technical error description, output a user-facing message that is clear, non-technical, and actionable.
hints: Tell the model: 'Write for a non-technical user — no HTTP codes or stack traces in the message.'
Require an action suggestion: 'End with what the user can do next.'
Ask for a 'friendly tone — frustrated users need calm reassurance.'

title: The Story Architect's Blueprint
narrativeDescription: In the Whispering Woods, every tale begins with a master blueprint — the story's bones laid out before a single word of narrative is spoken, ensuring the tale never loses its way.
task: Write a prompt that generates a short story outline. Input: genre, protagonist, and core conflict. Output: a 5-act structure with a one-paragraph description of each act and a proposed title.
hints: Define the 5 acts: Setup, Rising Action, Climax, Falling Action, Resolution.
Ask the model to weave the protagonist and conflict into each act description.
Request the title last — after the outline is complete.

title: The User Story Scribe
narrativeDescription: The Dragon Academy's project guild demands that every new feature be inscribed as a proper user story — persona named, desire stated, benefit declared — before a single stone of code is laid.
task: Write a prompt that converts a feature request description into a properly formatted Agile user story with acceptance criteria. Output: 'As a [persona], I want [goal] so that [benefit]' + 3-5 acceptance criteria as checkboxes.
hints: Enforce the exact user story format string in the prompt.
Ask for acceptance criteria as: '- [ ] Given X, when Y, then Z.'
Tell the model to infer the persona from the context if not stated.

title: The Test Forger's Trials
narrativeDescription: No enchantment leaves the Alchemist's Tower untested — the Test Forger inscribes every possible trial, edge case, and failure mode before the spell is cleared for use in the field.
task: Write a prompt that generates test cases for a given function signature and description. Output: a table with columns — Test ID, Input, Expected Output, Edge Case (Y/N), and Notes.
hints: Ask the model to start with happy-path tests, then boundary values, then error cases.
Specify the table format explicitly with all 5 column names.
Prompt for at least 2 edge cases per function.

title: The Commit Messenger's Rune
narrativeDescription: Each change inscribed in the Clockwork Citadel's code registry must carry a precise rune — a commit message that names the change, the reason, and the scope so future engineers can trace any gear back to its origin.
task: Write a prompt that generates a conventional commit message from a plain-language description of a code change. Output must follow the Conventional Commits spec: type(scope): subject, optional body, optional footer.
hints: Include the Conventional Commits type list: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore.
Ask the model to infer the type from the description.
Tell it to keep the subject line under 72 characters.

title: The Release Proclamation
narrativeDescription: When a new version of the Clockwork Citadel's machinery is unveiled, the Release Herald must proclaim it to all engineers and users — clear, structured, and exciting enough to read in full.
task: Write a prompt that generates release notes for a software version from a list of merged pull request titles. Audience: end users. Format: headline, 2-sentence summary, bullet list of user-facing changes.
hints: Tell the model to translate technical PR titles into user-friendly benefit statements.
Filter out internal/refactor PRs — only include user-facing changes.
Ask for an energetic headline: '🚀 Version X.Y is here' style (without emoji if preferred).

title: The Incident Chronicle
narrativeDescription: When a magical storm disrupts the Clockwork Citadel's operations, a precise incident report must be filed — timeline, root cause, and remediation — so the same disaster never strikes twice.
task: Write a prompt that generates a post-incident report from a timeline of events. Output: executive summary, timeline table, root cause analysis, impact summary, and action items with owners.
hints: Define the timeline table columns: Time / Event / Responsible Team.
Ask for the root cause in two parts: immediate cause and contributing factors.
Action items should follow format: 'Owner — Action — Target Date'.

title: The Chain of Reasoning Forge
narrativeDescription: The Oracle's deepest riddles cannot be solved in one leap — the Reasoning Forge breaks each puzzle into links of logic, forging a chain from premise to conclusion that no sophist can break.
task: Write a prompt that instructs an LLM to solve a multi-step word problem by showing its reasoning step by step before giving the final answer. The model must label each step and verify its answer at the end.
hints: Use the phrase 'Let's think step by step' or 'Work through this carefully before answering.'
Ask the model to label steps: 'Step 1:', 'Step 2:', etc.
Add a final instruction: 'After your final answer, verify it by checking against the original problem.'

title: The Mirror Persona Ritual
narrativeDescription: At the Shadow Realm's threshold, identity itself is malleable — the Mirror Ritual teaches an agent to fully inhabit any persona, from tone to vocabulary, so the mask becomes indistinguishable from the face.
task: Write a role prompt that turns an LLM into a grumpy but brilliant senior developer who gives brutally honest code reviews. It must stay in character when the user pushes back on the feedback.
hints: Describe the persona in two dimensions: personality (grumpy, blunt) and expertise (senior, 20 years).
Add a consistency instruction: 'Do not break character even if the user seems upset.'
Give an example of the persona's voice: 'e.g., This function is a mess. Here is why...'

title: The Example Weaver's Three Threads
narrativeDescription: At the Wizard School, the elder masters teach not by description but by demonstration — three perfect examples woven together so the apprentice absorbs the pattern without being told the rule.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that teaches an LLM to convert passive-voice sentences to active voice. Include 3 input/output example pairs, then prompt the model to apply the pattern to a new sentence.
hints: Choose examples that vary in sentence structure so the pattern generalizes.
Format examples as: 'Input: ... Output: ...' pairs.
After the examples, add: 'Now apply the same transformation to: [NEW SENTENCE]'

title: The Tone Calibrator's Pendulum
narrativeDescription: The Celestial Court's diplomatic scribes must shift tone like a pendulum — formal for the king, casual for the people, technical for the mages — all while delivering the same underlying message.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that rewrites a technical message in three tones: formal (executive), casual (team Slack), and technical (engineering doc). Provide 2 examples before prompting on a new input.
hints: Each example should show the same source text transformed into all three tones.
Label tones clearly in the example: 'Formal: / Casual: / Technical:'.
Pick examples with very different register gaps to make the pattern obvious.

title: The Enchanted Classification Lens
narrativeDescription: The Runic Library's sorting lens reads every new scroll arriving from the realm and assigns it to its rightful shelf — each category defined, each rule known, each ambiguous case handled gracefully.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that classifies customer support tickets into categories: Billing, Technical Issue, Account Access, Feature Request, or Other. Provide 3 labeled examples, then classify a new ticket.
hints: Choose examples that represent different categories — not all from one.
Add an 'Other' example to teach the model when to use the catch-all.
Format: 'Ticket: ... Category: ...' for each example.

title: The Frost Oracle's Forecast
narrativeDescription: The frost mountain seers study scattered signs — trade winds, beast migrations, celestial alignments — and reason through them systematically to forecast the season ahead with uncanny accuracy.
task: Write a prompt that asks an LLM to analyze a business scenario and forecast risks for the next quarter. It must reason through economic, competitive, and internal factors before stating its top 3 risks.
hints: Structure the reasoning: 'First assess economic factors, then competitive, then internal.'
Ask the model to rank risks by likelihood and impact.
End with: 'State your top 3 risks as: Risk / Likelihood / Impact / Mitigation suggestion.'

title: The Comparison Crystal
narrativeDescription: The Crystal Caves hold twin gems that reveal hidden truths when held side by side — the Comparison Crystal spell makes any two concepts reveal their similarities, differences, and hidden tensions at once.
task: Write a prompt that compares two competing technologies (e.g., REST vs GraphQL). Output: a structured comparison table, a 'when to use each' section, and a final recommendation paragraph.
hints: Define comparison dimensions in the prompt: performance, ease of use, tooling, use cases.
Ask for the table in markdown format with consistent row structure.
Tell the model to make a concrete recommendation — not just 'it depends'.

title: The Persona Compass
narrativeDescription: The Sea of Knowledge is vast and each traveler needs a guide suited to their nature — the Persona Compass ensures the guide speaks in the voice the traveler trusts most, whether sage, peer, or cheerful tutor.
task: Write three system prompt variants for a customer service bot: one formal/corporate tone, one friendly/casual tone, one empathetic/supportive tone. Highlight the key differences in each.
hints: Write all three prompts, then add a brief note under each on what makes it distinct.
Use specific word choices that signal each tone: 'assist' vs 'help out' vs 'here for you'.
Keep each prompt concise — the contrast should be in tone, not length.

title: The Lore Extractor's Sieve
narrativeDescription: Ancient tomes at the Runic Library contain centuries of lore, yet the master's sieve can shake free only the facts that answer a specific question — no chaff, no conjecture, only sourced truth.
task: Write a prompt that reads a long article (pasted by the user) and extracts only facts relevant to a specific question. Output: a numbered list of relevant facts with the sentence they were drawn from quoted.
hints: Tell the model: 'Only include facts directly relevant to the question — ignore tangential information.'
Require the source quote: 'After each fact, include Quote: exact sentence from text.'
Add: 'If no relevant facts exist, say: No relevant facts found in the provided text.'

title: The Whispering Translation Engine
narrativeDescription: The Whispering Woods hold knowledge in a hundred tongues, and the sacred translation engine must bridge not just words but entire registers — from the philosopher's Greek to the merchant's pidgin.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that translates formal academic English sentences into plain English (8th-grade reading level). Include 2 examples before prompting on a new academic sentence.
hints: Choose examples where the academic original is noticeably dense and the plain version is genuinely simpler.
Tell the model: 'Target a reading level of 8th grade — no jargon, short sentences.'
Format: 'Academic: ... Plain: ...' for each example pair.

title: The Iron Contract Negotiator
narrativeDescription: In the celestial court's trading chambers, no deal is struck without the Iron Contract — the negotiator's grimoire lists every term, every concession, and every fallback before a single handshake is offered.
task: Write a role prompt that casts an LLM as a negotiation coach helping a freelancer negotiate rates with a client. It must roleplay the client's side, give feedback after each exchange, and suggest stronger counteroffers.
hints: Describe two roles in the prompt: the client persona the model plays, and the coach role it also holds.
Tell the model to break character after each exchange to give coaching feedback.
Seed a realistic client attitude: 'The client is budget-conscious but willing to pay for quality.'

title: The Telescope of Hidden Patterns
narrativeDescription: The Starfall Observatory's telescopes do not just show stars — they reveal the patterns no mortal eye can see. The reasoning lens turns raw numbers into insight through systematic analysis.
task: Write a prompt that analyzes a table of sales data and identifies trends. It must reason through monthly patterns, anomalies, and potential causes before providing a 3-point executive insight summary.
hints: Ask the model to narrate its analysis step by step before summarizing.
Define 'anomaly': 'Any month where growth or decline exceeds 20%.'
Executive summary format: 'Insight 1: ... Insight 2: ... Insight 3: ...'

title: The Few-Shot Sentiment Sculptor
narrativeDescription: The Starfall Observatory's sculptors shape raw emotional clay into precise forms — not just positive or negative, but the full nuanced spectrum of human feeling, learned from a handful of perfect examples.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that classifies product reviews into fine-grained sentiments: delighted, satisfied, neutral, disappointed, or frustrated. Provide 4 labeled examples before prompting on a new review.
hints: Spread examples across at least 4 of the 5 categories to prevent bias.
Pick examples that are ambiguous enough to show the model the decision boundary.
Format: 'Review: ... Sentiment: ...' for consistency.

title: The Constraint Sculptor's Chisel
narrativeDescription: The master sculptors of the Crystal Caves work only with constraint — every unnecessary word is chipped away, every vague instruction sharpened, until the shape of the perfect prompt emerges from the stone.
task: Write a prompt that rewrites a vague, rambling prompt given by the user into a precise, well-structured prompt. The output must show: the rewritten prompt, a list of improvements made, and a one-line rationale for each improvement.
hints: Tell the model to identify the core intent of the vague prompt before rewriting.
Improvements list format: 'Change: X — Reason: Y'.
Ask it to check for: ambiguous scope, missing constraints, unclear output format, and undefined audience.

title: The Mind Labyrinth Navigator
narrativeDescription: The Mind Labyrinth's deepest chambers hold answers that only emerge from long chains of reasoning — the navigator must trace every corridor, mark every dead end, and arrive at truth through the correct passage.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that solves a logic puzzle by reasoning step by step, identifying contradictions, and eliminating wrong answers before stating the correct one.
hints: Ask the model to list all possible answers first, then eliminate them one by one.
Use the phrase: 'Do not jump to the answer. Work through each possibility.'
Ask it to call out the elimination reason for each rejected option.

title: The Time Archives' Lost Context
narrativeDescription: The Time Archives hold records of events in fragments — each fragment is context for the next. The archivist must teach the oracle to use prior context wisely rather than answering each question in isolation.
task: Write a system prompt that instructs an LLM to maintain conversation context across a multi-turn customer support dialogue. It must reference previous turns when relevant and not ask for information already given.
hints: Add a rule: 'Never ask for information the user has already provided in this conversation.'
Tell the model to summarize what it knows about the issue at each turn start if the issue is complex.
Instruct it to say 'Based on what you told me earlier...' to signal context use.

title: The Wizard School Lesson Planner
narrativeDescription: At the Wizard School, no lesson is improvised — the curriculum council inscribes every lesson plan in advance, with objectives, activities, and assessments so any substitute teacher can deliver a masterclass.
task: Write a prompt that generates a complete lesson plan for a 45-minute class on a given topic. Output: learning objectives (3), materials needed, step-by-step agenda with time allocations, and one formative assessment activity.
hints: Tell the model the target audience: 'High school students, mixed prior knowledge.'
Agenda format: '0:00–0:10 — Introduction activity (10 min)'.
Formative assessment should be quick: exit ticket, think-pair-share, or a 2-minute quiz.

title: The Emerald Bazaar's Price Whisperer
narrativeDescription: The Emerald Bazaar's price whisperers see beyond the surface of any listed item and compose dynamic pricing descriptions that make value self-evident — not just what something costs, but why it is worth it.
task: Write a prompt that rewrites flat price-list text into value-focused pricing page copy for a SaaS product. Each tier must lead with the customer benefit, not the feature list.
hints: Tell the model: 'Lead every tier with the primary benefit, not a plan name or price.'
Ask it to transform feature language: 'Unlimited users' → 'Scale your team without limits'.
Request a one-line customer persona statement per tier.

title: The Shadow Detective's Deduction
narrativeDescription: In the Shadow Realm's investigation chambers, every clue speaks if you know how to question it. The Shadow Detective reasons through evidence methodically, never leaping to a conclusion before every fact is weighed.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt for root cause analysis of a system outage. Given a set of symptoms and logs, the model must reason through possible causes, eliminate unlikely ones, and state the most probable root cause with confidence.
hints: Ask the model to list hypotheses first, then test each against the evidence.
Define 'confidence': 'Rate each hypothesis Low/Medium/High confidence before eliminating.'
End with a final statement: 'Most probable root cause: ... Confidence: ... Next step: ...'

title: The Forest Cartographer's Context Map
narrativeDescription: In the Enchanted Forest, no two trees look alike but all are connected. The cartographer's spell layers context upon context until the reader understands not just the forest but why each tree grows where it does.
task: Write a prompt that provides rich context about a company (industry, product, audience, tone) before asking the model to generate a blog post introduction. Demonstrate how adding layered context improves output quality.
hints: Provide context in clear labeled blocks: Company / Product / Target Audience / Tone / Goal.
After the context block, add the specific task: 'Write a 100-word blog intro on topic X.'
Compare outputs with and without context to see the difference.

title: The Iron Forge's Code Explainer
narrativeDescription: At the Iron Forge, every gear's purpose must be inscribed before it is installed — no craftsman should wield a mechanism they do not understand. The explanation ritual translates code into craft knowledge.
task: Write a prompt that explains a given code snippet in plain English to a non-technical stakeholder. It must describe what the code does, what problem it solves, and any risks if it breaks — with zero technical jargon.
hints: Tell the model: 'Your audience is a product manager who does not code.'
Define jargon ban: 'Do not use terms like function, variable, loop, or null.'
Structure: What it does / What problem it solves / What breaks if it fails.

title: The Celestial Court's Protocol Keeper
narrativeDescription: At the Celestial Court, the Protocol Keeper ensures every agent in the hall follows the rules of engagement — never overstepping their role, always deferring to the correct authority in the correct order.
task: Write a system prompt for a multi-step agent that collects user requirements, confirms understanding, drafts a document, and only finalizes it after explicit user approval. Define each stage and transition rule.
hints: Define the 4 stages explicitly: Collect / Confirm / Draft / Finalize.
Add a transition rule: 'Do not move to the next stage until the user explicitly approves the current one.'
Include a fallback: 'If the user rejects the draft, return to Collect and ask what to change.'

title: The Runic Library's Self-Critic
narrativeDescription: The Runic Library's most advanced scribes do not just write — they critique their own work before submitting it, checking for flaws, omissions, and improvements in a second pass of the same quill.
task: Write a prompt that instructs an LLM to generate a first draft of a persuasive paragraph, then critique it against three criteria (clarity, evidence, call to action), then write an improved second draft.
hints: Structure explicitly: 'Draft 1 → Self-Critique → Draft 2'.
Define the three criteria numerically: '1. Clarity 2. Evidence 3. Call to Action'.
Tell the model: 'In the critique, be specific about what is weak and why.'

title: The Floating Island's Agent Dispatcher
narrativeDescription: On the Floating Islands, the grand dispatcher coordinates a fleet of specialist agents — each with a single mastery — routing every task to the right agent at the right time so no skill is wasted.
task: Write a meta-prompt that describes an agent routing system: given a user request, the orchestrator agent must classify the request type and route it to the correct specialist sub-agent (writer, coder, analyst, or researcher).
hints: Define each specialist's scope in one sentence within the prompt.
Add routing rules: 'If the request involves code, route to Coder. If it involves data, route to Analyst.'
Include an 'Unknown' fallback: 'If unsure, ask the user one clarifying question before routing.'

title: The Time Archives' Memory Keeper
narrativeDescription: The Time Archives must never forget — the Memory Keeper's ritual ensures that information gathered in one session is preserved and surfaced precisely when needed in the next, making the archive ever more powerful over time.
task: Write a system prompt for a persistent research assistant agent that maintains a knowledge base of facts the user provides, retrieves relevant facts when answering new questions, and tells the user when it is relying on stored memory.
hints: Define memory behavior: 'When the user states a fact, store it labeled as [USER FACT].'
Add retrieval instruction: 'When answering, first check stored facts for relevance.'
Transparency rule: 'If using stored memory, say: Based on what you told me previously...'

title: The Iron Citadel's Budget Ledger
narrativeDescription: The Iron Citadel's treasurer demands a clear accounting of the realm's coffers. Only a prompt that specifies format and constraints will yield a usable budget plan.
task: Write a prompt that asks an AI to create a monthly budget plan for a small business with $50,000 revenue, specifying that the output must include fixed costs, variable costs, and a savings target.
hints: State the revenue figure and business type explicitly in the prompt.
Ask for specific line items like rent, payroll, and marketing.
Specify that totals must sum correctly to the given revenue.

title: Dream Weaver's Risk Oracle
narrativeDescription: In the Dream Weaver's Sanctum, visions of financial ruin flicker — but only clear thinking separates real risk from illusion. Guide the oracle to assess investment risk step by step.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that instructs an AI to assess the risk profile of investing in emerging market bonds, walking through each risk factor (currency, political, liquidity, credit) before giving a final verdict.
hints: Explicitly tell the AI to analyze each risk category in order before concluding.
Use phrases like 'First, consider… then…' to guide the reasoning sequence.
Ask for a final summary rating only after all factors are examined.

title: The Astral Library's Compliance Scroll
narrativeDescription: The Astral Library holds every law ever written, but the scholar must know which tome to open. Craft a role prompt that transforms the AI into a compliance expert.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the persona of a senior compliance officer reviewing a new employee onboarding policy for GDPR compliance, asking it to flag any violations.
hints: Define the persona's seniority and domain expertise explicitly.
Include the policy text or a placeholder instruction to paste it in.
Ask the AI to cite specific GDPR articles for each flag.

title: Coral Kingdom's Contract Codex
narrativeDescription: Beneath the Coral Kingdom, ancient contracts bind merfolk and surface traders alike. The right prompt unlocks a contract review that misses nothing.
task: Write a prompt to have an AI review a vendor contract and extract: payment terms, termination clauses, liability caps, and any unusual or risky provisions.
hints: List each extraction target as a numbered requirement.
Ask the AI to quote the relevant contract text for each finding.
Specify a structured output with one section per category.

title: The Volcanic Forge's Audit Report
narrativeDescription: From the Volcanic Forge, molten truths are hammered into audit reports — each finding must be precise, sourced, and actionable. Only a well-structured prompt shapes the metal correctly.
task: Write a prompt instructing an AI to generate an internal audit report for a fictional company's expense reimbursement process, including executive summary, findings, risk ratings, and recommendations.
hints: Define the exact section headings you want in the output.
Ask for a risk rating scale (e.g., High/Medium/Low) and instruct it to apply it.
Specify a word limit per section to keep the report tight.

title: Moonlit Bazaar's Tax Guidance Scroll
narrativeDescription: At the Moonlit Bazaar, traders from a dozen realms exchange gold and secrets — and every transaction has a tax. A few well-chosen examples teach the oracle how to handle edge cases.
task: Write a few-shot prompt that shows the AI three examples of freelancer tax scenarios and their correct deduction advice, then asks it to handle a fourth novel scenario.
hints: Make each example follow the same input-output format.
Choose examples that cover different edge cases (home office, equipment, travel).
Ensure the fourth scenario differs meaningfully from all three examples.

title: The Sky Temple's Research Synthesis
narrativeDescription: High in the Sky Temple, scholars gather scrolls from a hundred disciplines and distill them into single truths. Craft a prompt that synthesizes a literature review from multiple sources.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that provides three research abstract excerpts on remote work productivity and asks the AI to synthesize them into a coherent two-paragraph literature review.
hints: Paste the abstracts as labeled context blocks (e.g., [Source 1], [Source 2]).
Instruct the AI to identify areas of agreement and contradiction.
Ask for in-text citations referencing the source labels you provided.

title: Runic Caverns' Hypothesis Stone
narrativeDescription: Deep in the Runic Caverns, ancient hypotheses are carved into stone — each one a testable claim waiting for proof. Use chain-of-thought to help the AI generate a research hypothesis correctly.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that guides an AI through generating a scientific hypothesis about the effect of sleep deprivation on decision-making, requiring it to identify variables, state assumptions, and form a falsifiable claim.
hints: Ask the AI to identify independent, dependent, and control variables first.
Require it to state its background assumptions before drafting the hypothesis.
Insist the final claim be falsifiable and measurable.

title: Enchanted Academy's Persona Scroll
narrativeDescription: At the Enchanted Academy, every student learns to wear a different face — but a persona must be consistent and believable. Role-prompt the AI to become a specific user archetype.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the persona of a 35-year-old first-time homebuyer with a limited budget and asks it to evaluate a mortgage offer from that perspective.
hints: Define the persona's financial situation, goals, and pain points explicitly.
Ask the AI to respond in first person as the persona.
Include specific concerns the persona would realistically have.

title: The Time Weaver's SWOT Loom
narrativeDescription: On the Time Weaver's Loom, past failures and future opportunities are woven together into strategy. A structured output prompt produces a clean SWOT analysis.
task: Write a prompt that asks an AI to produce a SWOT analysis for a fictional SaaS startup entering the project management tool market, using a 2x2 table format.
hints: Specify the exact table format: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats as headers.
Give 2-3 facts about the startup as context before asking for the analysis.
Ask for 3-5 bullet points per quadrant.

title: Emerald Throne's OKR Decree
narrativeDescription: From the Emerald Throne, decrees shape kingdoms — but only well-formed OKRs translate ambition into measurable progress. Craft the prompt that writes them correctly.
task: Write a prompt that instructs an AI to write three OKRs for a product team focused on improving user retention, ensuring each objective has three key results that are measurable and time-bound.
hints: Specify that each Key Result must include a numeric target and a deadline.
Ask the AI to focus on lagging indicators that directly reflect retention.
Request that objectives be aspirational but achievable within one quarter.

title: Frost Oracle's KPI Revelation
narrativeDescription: The Frost Oracle sees through the ice of vanity metrics to the true signals beneath. A chain-of-thought prompt uncovers the KPIs that actually matter.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that asks an AI to determine the top 5 KPIs for a subscription e-commerce business, requiring it to first identify business goals, then link each KPI to a goal, before presenting the final list.
hints: Instruct the AI to explicitly state the business goals before listing any KPIs.
Ask it to explain the causal link between each KPI and its parent goal.
Request a prioritization rationale for why these 5 over alternatives.

title: Storm Peaks' Incident Response Codex
narrativeDescription: When the Storm Peaks rage, the unprepared perish — but those with a clear incident response plan survive. Provide enough context for the AI to draft a realistic plan.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a fintech company's infrastructure (cloud-hosted, 50k daily users, payment processing) and asks the AI to draft a P1 incident response plan.
hints: Include details about team size, on-call rotation, and escalation paths in the context.
Specify the output structure: detection, triage, communication, resolution, post-mortem.
Mention compliance constraints (e.g., PCI-DSS) that affect the response procedure.

title: Golden Archives' Changelog Scroll
narrativeDescription: The Golden Archives preserve every change made to the realm's systems — but a changelog only has value if it is clear and consistent. Few examples set the standard.
task: Write a few-shot prompt with two changelog entry examples that teaches the AI the correct format (version, date, categories: Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed), then ask it to generate an entry for a new feature.
hints: Make both example entries follow Keep a Changelog conventions exactly.
Vary the categories used across the two examples to cover different cases.
Provide a brief feature description for the AI to convert into the new entry.

title: Mind Palace's Competitive Intelligence
narrativeDescription: In the Mind Palace, every piece of intelligence has its place — but competitive analysis requires a structured mind. Role-prompt the AI as a market analyst to extract insights correctly.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of a senior market analyst and asks it to perform a competitive analysis of three fictional CRM vendors, comparing pricing, features, and target segments.
hints: Give the analyst a specific mandate: recommend which vendor a mid-market B2B company should choose.
Provide basic vendor names and one distinguishing fact each as context.
Ask for a comparison table plus a written recommendation.

title: Shadow Library's Style Guide Tome
narrativeDescription: The Shadow Library preserves voices that would otherwise be lost — but only a brand voice guide ensures every writer speaks as one. Craft a prompt that produces this guide.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to create a brand voice guide for a fintech startup that wants to sound trustworthy yet approachable, including tone, vocabulary dos/don'ts, and three example phrases.
hints: Describe the target audience and their trust pain points in the prompt.
Ask for a 'words to use' and 'words to avoid' list.
Request that the example phrases cover different contexts: homepage, error message, success message.

title: Crystal Forest's A/B Test Design
narrativeDescription: In the Crystal Forest, every path looks the same until you measure which one leads home. Chain-of-thought reasoning ensures a sound A/B test design.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that asks an AI to design an A/B test for a checkout page CTA button, requiring it to state the hypothesis, define success metrics, calculate sample size rationale, and outline the test duration.
hints: Instruct the AI to state its hypothesis in 'if X then Y because Z' format.
Ask it to justify the sample size with a conversion rate assumption.
Require it to identify potential confounding factors before finalizing the design.

title: Wandering Isle's User Research Synthesis
narrativeDescription: The Wandering Isle collects stories from every port — but only structured synthesis turns raw user interviews into insight. Provide rich context for the AI to summarize findings.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that includes five brief user interview quotes about a mobile banking app and asks the AI to synthesize them into key themes, pain points, and product recommendations.
hints: Label each quote with a participant ID and brief demographic.
Ask the AI to group themes by frequency and severity.
Request that each recommendation link back to at least one quote.

title: Clockwork Observatory's Sprint Planning
narrativeDescription: In the Clockwork Observatory, every gear must mesh perfectly — and sprint planning is the art of fitting the right tasks into the right week. A role prompt focuses the AI on the planning role.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of an experienced Scrum Master and asks it to facilitate a sprint planning session for a 5-person team by suggesting how to break down a large feature into sprint-sized stories.
hints: Describe the large feature and the team's velocity as context.
Ask the AI to apply the INVEST criteria when breaking down stories.
Request story point estimates and a suggested sprint goal.

title: Aurora Sanctum's Performance Review
narrativeDescription: Beneath the auroras, each soul's contributions glow differently — and a fair performance review captures that light in words. Few-shot examples calibrate the AI's tone.
task: Write a few-shot prompt with two performance review examples (one high-performer, one needs-improvement) and ask the AI to write a balanced review for a mid-level engineer who meets expectations.
hints: Ensure both examples use the same structure: strengths, areas for growth, overall rating.
Make the 'needs-improvement' example constructive, not punitive, to set the right tone.
Provide 3-4 bullet points of factual accomplishments for the target engineer.

title: Iron Citadel's Architecture Decision Record
narrativeDescription: Every stone of the Iron Citadel was laid by decision — and each decision was recorded in the Architect's Codex. A precise context prompt produces a proper ADR.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a decision to migrate from a monolithic architecture to microservices and asks the AI to produce an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) with context, decision, status, and consequences.
hints: Provide the current system's pain points as context (e.g., deployment bottlenecks, team scaling issues).
Ask for alternatives considered and reasons they were rejected.
Specify the ADR format: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences.

title: Dream Weaver's Ad Copy Spell
narrativeDescription: The Dream Weaver knows that the right words plant desire in the listener's mind. Role-prompting the AI as a copywriter unlocks persuasive ad copy that converts.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that casts the AI as a senior direct-response copywriter and asks it to write three headline variants for a landing page selling a productivity app to busy professionals.
hints: Specify the target audience's primary frustration and desired outcome.
Ask for one headline focused on pain, one on gain, one on fear of missing out.
Request a one-sentence rationale for each headline variant.

title: Astral Library's Patent Prior Art Search
narrativeDescription: The Astral Library contains every invention ever conceived — and finding prior art is a matter of asking the right questions in the right order. Chain-of-thought guides the search.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that asks an AI to conduct a conceptual patent prior art search for an invention (a wearable hydration monitor), requiring it to decompose the invention into components, identify search keywords per component, and assess novelty.
hints: Ask the AI to first list the distinct technical components of the invention.
Require it to generate 3-5 search terms per component before assessing novelty.
Ask for a final novelty assessment with reasoning based on the component analysis.

title: Coral Kingdom's SLA Covenant
narrativeDescription: The Coral Kingdom's trade agreements hold firm because every clause is clear and every metric measurable. Prompt the AI to draft an SLA that leaves nothing ambiguous.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to draft a Service Level Agreement for a B2B cloud storage provider, covering uptime guarantees, response time targets, escalation procedures, and remedies for breaches.
hints: Specify the exact sections you want as named headings.
Ask for numeric thresholds (e.g., 99.9% uptime, 4-hour response).
Request that remedies be expressed as service credits with a formula.

title: Volcanic Forge's RFC Draft
narrativeDescription: In the Volcanic Forge, proposals are hammered until they are airtight — an RFC that survives the forge can survive any review. A well-contexted prompt produces one.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that provides background on a team's plan to replace a REST API with GraphQL and asks the AI to draft an RFC with problem statement, proposed solution, trade-offs, and rollout plan.
hints: Include the team size, current API usage patterns, and consumer count as context.
Ask for a trade-offs section that lists at least two cons alongside the pros.
Request a phased rollout plan with rollback criteria.

title: Moonlit Bazaar's Market Research Brief
narrativeDescription: At the Moonlit Bazaar, every merchant knows their customer — and a market research brief is the map that leads there. Basic prompt construction shapes the output effectively.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to produce a market research brief for launching a meal-kit delivery service in a mid-sized city, covering target segments, size estimates, competitor landscape, and entry barriers.
hints: Specify the city type and demographic profile you are targeting.
Ask for a brief competitor table with at least three players.
Request a one-paragraph executive summary at the top.

title: Sky Temple's Experiment Design Altar
narrativeDescription: On the Sky Temple's altar, experiments are offered to the gods of data — only those with sound designs receive answers. Role-prompting the AI as a data scientist sharpens the design.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the persona of a senior data scientist and asks it to design an experiment to test whether a new onboarding flow reduces time-to-first-value for SaaS users.
hints: Ask the data scientist persona to define the primary metric and guardrail metrics.
Require a randomization strategy and control/treatment group specification.
Ask for a minimum detectable effect size and confidence level.

title: Runic Caverns' Safety Procedure Inscription
narrativeDescription: The Runic Caverns hold the oldest safety procedures — carved in stone because lives depend on clarity. A structured output prompt ensures the procedure is complete and usable.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to generate a workplace safety procedure for operating a forklift in a warehouse, structured with a purpose statement, required PPE, step-by-step operating procedure, and emergency actions.
hints: Specify that each step in the operating procedure must be numbered and imperative.
Ask for a separate section on pre-operation inspection checklist.
Request that emergency actions be in bold or clearly marked for quick reference.

title: Enchanted Academy's Promotion Case
narrativeDescription: At the Enchanted Academy, advancement is earned through evidence — and a promotion case that lacks structure falls on deaf ears. Chain-of-thought builds it correctly.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that guides an AI through writing a promotion case for a software engineer moving from mid-level to senior, requiring it to first identify the level-up criteria, then map accomplishments to criteria, then draft the narrative.
hints: Tell the AI to start by listing typical senior engineer criteria at most tech companies.
Provide 4-5 accomplishment bullet points and ask it to map each to a criterion.
Ask for the final narrative to be written in third person.

title: Time Weaver's Retrospective Facilitation
narrativeDescription: The Time Weaver looks back on every sprint to find the threads worth keeping. A context-rich prompt helps the AI facilitate a retrospective that surfaces real insights.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a sprint that missed its goal due to scope creep and integration delays, and asks the AI to facilitate a retrospective using the Start/Stop/Continue format.
hints: Include the sprint goal, what was completed, and what was left unfinished as context.
Mention team size and any interpersonal friction as background.
Ask the AI to generate 2-3 action items for each Start/Stop/Continue category.

title: Emerald Throne's Diversity Statement
narrativeDescription: The Emerald Throne rules a diverse realm — and its declarations must be sincere, specific, and free of hollow platitudes. A role prompt focused on authenticity produces the right statement.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of a People & Culture lead and asks it to draft an authentic diversity and inclusion statement for a 200-person tech company, avoiding clichés.
hints: Ask the AI persona to explicitly flag and replace any hollow buzzwords it might be tempted to use.
Provide two concrete D&I initiatives the company has already implemented as context.
Request a statement under 200 words that references specific actions.

title: Frost Oracle's Statistical Interpretation
narrativeDescription: The Frost Oracle reads the numbers carved in ice — but numbers alone tell no story. A chain-of-thought prompt ensures statistical results are interpreted correctly before conclusions are drawn.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that asks an AI to interpret the results of a t-test (given p-value, confidence interval, and effect size), requiring it to assess statistical significance, practical significance, and limitations before recommending action.
hints: Provide concrete hypothetical test results (e.g., p=0.04, Cohen's d=0.2, 95% CI [0.1, 0.9]).
Ask the AI to distinguish between statistical significance and practical significance explicitly.
Require it to name at least two limitations before recommending an action.

title: Storm Peaks' Disaster Recovery Plan
narrativeDescription: When the Storm Peaks unleash their fury, only those with a tested disaster recovery plan survive intact. Rich context enables the AI to write one that actually works.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a retail e-commerce company with a 4-hour RTO and 1-hour RPO, and asks the AI to draft a disaster recovery plan covering backup strategy, failover procedure, and communication plan.
hints: Specify the hosting environment (e.g., AWS multi-region) and critical systems in the context.
Ask for a runbook-style procedure with numbered steps for the failover.
Request a stakeholder communication template as part of the output.

title: Golden Archives' Training Material
narrativeDescription: The Golden Archives pass knowledge across generations — but only well-structured training material survives the test of time. A few examples calibrate the AI's instructional design.
task: Write a few-shot prompt with two examples of module summaries from a corporate training program (different topics, same format), then ask the AI to write a module summary for data privacy best practices.
hints: Each example should include: module title, learning objectives, key concepts, and a quiz question.
Use different subject areas in the two examples to show format generality.
For the target module, specify the audience (e.g., non-technical staff) to guide the tone.

title: Mind Palace's Data Analysis Plan
narrativeDescription: In the Mind Palace, every analysis has a room prepared before the data arrives. A role-prompted statistician lays out the analysis plan before a single number is crunched.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of a research statistician and asks it to write a data analysis plan for a survey study measuring employee engagement, specifying variables, statistical tests, and visualizations.
hints: Ask the statistician persona to list each research question and the corresponding test for it.
Specify that the survey uses a 5-point Likert scale to inform the choice of tests.
Request a section on handling missing data and outliers.

title: Shadow Library's Deprecation Notice
narrativeDescription: The Shadow Library marks obsolete tomes with a clear deprecation notice so scholars know what to avoid. A precise prompt produces a notice that informs without alarming.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to draft a deprecation notice for a legacy REST API endpoint that will be sunset in 6 months, addressed to external developer partners, covering timeline, migration path, and support resources.
hints: Ask for a subject line, body, and call-to-action in the notice.
Specify the replacement endpoint and its documentation link as context.
Ask for a friendly but clear tone that conveys urgency without panic.

title: Crystal Forest's Landing Page Copy
narrativeDescription: In the Crystal Forest, every path leads somewhere — and a landing page must guide the visitor to one destination: conversion. A structured output prompt shapes the copy correctly.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to generate a full landing page copy structure for a B2B cybersecurity tool, including hero headline, subheadline, three value proposition sections, social proof placeholder, and a CTA.
hints: Define each section as a labeled block in the output.
Specify the target buyer persona (e.g., CISOs at mid-market companies).
Ask for the CTA to be action-oriented and repeated twice on the page.

title: Wandering Isle's Product Roadmap
narrativeDescription: The Wandering Isle changes course with the tides — but a product roadmap gives the crew a shared destination. Context sets the strategic backdrop for a realistic roadmap.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a B2B SaaS company's current product state, top customer requests, and engineering capacity, then asks the AI to generate a 3-quarter product roadmap with themes and key initiatives.
hints: List 5-6 customer requests with rough priority signals as context.
Specify engineering team size and velocity as capacity constraints.
Ask the AI to group initiatives into quarterly themes rather than feature lists.

title: Clockwork Observatory's Pattern Documentation
narrativeDescription: In the Clockwork Observatory, recurring mechanisms are catalogued so they need not be reinvented. A role-prompted architect documents the pattern clearly for reuse.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of a software architect and asks it to document the Repository Pattern in a design pattern catalog entry format, including intent, applicability, structure, and example code snippet.
hints: Specify the catalog entry structure explicitly: Intent, Applicability, Participants, Code Example.
Ask the architect persona to include when NOT to use the pattern.
Request a minimal Java or Python code snippet to illustrate the pattern.

title: Aurora Sanctum's Salary Negotiation Script
narrativeDescription: In the Aurora Sanctum, negotiators are trained to speak with conviction and data. A chain-of-thought prompt builds a salary negotiation script that is strategic, not desperate.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that guides an AI through building a salary negotiation script for a software engineer with 5 years of experience, requiring it to first anchor a market rate, then identify leverage points, then draft the opening statement.
hints: Ask the AI to cite a rationale for the market rate anchor (e.g., survey data, competing offers).
Require it to list leverage points (skills, offer timing, role scarcity) before drafting.
Ask for the opening statement to be concise, confident, and specific.

title: Iron Citadel's Employee Handbook Chapter
narrativeDescription: The Iron Citadel's rulebook shapes the behavior of every soldier — and an employee handbook chapter must be clear, fair, and legally sound. A precise prompt produces the right text.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to draft a 'Remote Work Policy' chapter for an employee handbook, covering eligibility, equipment responsibilities, expected availability, and performance standards.
hints: Specify the company type and size to ground the policy in reality.
Ask for numbered policy items under each sub-section heading.
Request a final note about how exceptions to the policy are handled.

title: Dream Weaver's Trademark Analysis
narrativeDescription: The Dream Weaver knows that a name can haunt you — or empower you. Chain-of-thought reasoning walks through a trademark viability check before a brand is born.
task: Write a chain-of-thought prompt that asks an AI to evaluate the trademark viability of the brand name 'Luminos' for a lighting company, requiring it to check descriptiveness, similarity to existing marks, and distinctiveness before giving a verdict.
hints: Ask the AI to first assess whether the name is descriptive, suggestive, arbitrary, or fanciful.
Require it to consider phonetic and visual similarity to known brand names.
Ask for a final risk rating (high/medium/low) with a one-paragraph rationale.

title: Astral Library's Scientific Paper Summary
narrativeDescription: The Astral Library holds a thousand papers that no scholar has time to read — a precise summary prompt unlocks their secrets for the time-pressed researcher.
task: Write a few-shot prompt with one example of a scientific paper summary (abstract in, structured summary out), then ask the AI to summarize a second paper about the effects of microplastics on marine biodiversity.
hints: The example output should include: research question, methods, key findings, limitations.
Keep the example abstract short (4-5 sentences) so the format is easy to follow.
Provide a real or realistic abstract for the microplastics paper as the test input.

title: Coral Kingdom's Conflict Resolution Guide
narrativeDescription: In the Coral Kingdom, disputes between clans are resolved by a mediator who listens before speaking. A role-prompted mediator drafts a conflict resolution guide rooted in practice.
task: Write a role-prompting prompt that assigns the AI the role of a certified workplace mediator and asks it to write a step-by-step conflict resolution guide for managers handling team interpersonal disputes.
hints: Ask the mediator persona to ground each step in a named conflict resolution model (e.g., Interest-Based Relational approach).
Request 5-7 steps with a rationale for each.
Ask for a section on when to escalate to HR or legal.

title: Volcanic Forge's Migration Plan Scroll
narrativeDescription: The Volcanic Forge reshapes metal into new forms — but a database migration without a plan leaves slag. Context-driven prompting produces a migration plan that survives the heat.
task: Write a context-crafting prompt that describes a company migrating from an on-premise Oracle DB to PostgreSQL on AWS, and asks the AI to produce a migration plan with phases, risks, testing strategy, and rollback plan.
hints: Include database size, number of dependent applications, and downtime tolerance as context.
Ask for a phased plan: assessment, schema conversion, data migration, validation, cutover.
Request that each phase have explicit success criteria before proceeding to the next.

title: Moonlit Bazaar's Product Spec
narrativeDescription: At the Moonlit Bazaar, a merchant who cannot describe their product sells nothing. A design spec produced by a well-prompted AI gives every engineer a clear blueprint.
task: Write a prompt asking an AI to write a product design specification for a 'quick reply' feature in a mobile messaging app, covering user stories, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, and open questions.
hints: Structure the output with numbered sections matching the four required areas.
Ask for user stories in 'As a [user], I want [action] so that [benefit]' format.
Request that open questions include the decision owner and deadline.

title: Sky Temple's System Design Document
narrativeDescription: At the Sky Temple's apex, system designs are inscribed on clouds that all can read. A structured output prompt ensures no section of the system design is overlooked.
