hints: Instruct the model to identify the root component first, then enumerate child components level by level before adding props.
For interactive states, specify: 'Infer only states visually evident (hover highlighted, disabled grayed) — do not invent states not shown.'
Use a consistent component node format: {name, type, props: {}, children: [], accessibility_note: ''} as the output schema.

title: Eternal Flame Crucible: Tool Composition Specification
narrativeDescription: The Eternal Flame Crucible forges agents that know not only what tools exist but how to compose them into chains of power. Master tool composition specification for complex agentic tasks.
task: Design a prompt that defines a set of 5 tools with their signatures and constraints, then instructs an agent to solve a multi-step problem by composing those tools in sequence — specifying tool call order, input/output chaining logic, and error handling if a tool returns null or fails. Document the full tool composition plan before execution.
hints: Define each tool with: name, description, input_schema, output_schema, and constraints — so the agent knows exactly what it can and cannot do.
Require a planning step before any tool is called: 'First, output the full tool call sequence as a numbered plan with expected inputs and outputs.'
Define error handling explicitly: 'If tool X returns null, fall back to tool Y with the following adjusted input schema...'

title: Dimensional Thought Weaver: API Orchestration Prompt
narrativeDescription: The Dimensional Thought Weaver stitches together calls across realms — each API a thread in its infinite tapestry. Build a prompt that orchestrates multi-API workflows with precision.
task: Design an agent prompt that orchestrates a workflow requiring calls to 3 different APIs (e.g., search, data enrichment, notification): the agent must plan the call sequence, handle rate limits and partial failures gracefully, and produce a structured execution log alongside the final output. Define the agent's decision logic for each API interaction.
hints: Include API constraints in the prompt: rate limits, required authentication headers, and known failure modes the agent must anticipate.
Require the agent to emit a structured log entry after each API call: {api, status, latency_ms, result_summary, next_action}.
Define escalation logic: 'If 2 or more APIs fail, halt execution and output a structured failure report instead of guessing a result.'

title: Legend of the Final Prompt: Code Generation + Test Agent
narrativeDescription: The Legend of the Final Prompt ends with an agent that writes code and immediately proves it works. Build the ultimate code generation and test agent that validates its own output.
task: Design an agent prompt that receives a function specification, generates the implementation, writes a comprehensive test suite covering happy path and edge cases, identifies potential failure modes in the implementation, and outputs a self-assessment of coverage completeness. The agent must not output a final answer until the test suite passes its own internal review.
hints: Structure the agent's pipeline as explicit phases: (1) Implement, (2) Write tests, (3) Trace through tests mentally, (4) Fix failures, (5) Report.
Instruct the agent: 'In Phase 3, manually execute each test case step-by-step in your reasoning — do not assume the code is correct.'
Define coverage completeness as a checklist: happy path, null/empty input, boundary values, type errors — the agent must check each explicitly.

title: The Eternal Codex Supreme Trial
narrativeDescription: The Eternal Codex demands a master who can forge prompts that judge themselves. Only one who wields meta-prompting and self-evaluation in unison may claim its secrets.
task: Design a meta-prompt that autonomously generates 5 distinct prompt variants for a given task, then self-evaluates each variant using a structured scoring rubric (clarity, specificity, bias risk) embedded within the same prompt. Combine meta-prompting with self-evaluation by instructing the model to produce the variants and immediately critique them in a second reasoning pass. Output must include a ranked list with scores and a final recommended variant. This mission requires you to master the loop of generation and introspective judgment within a single prompt.
hints: Structure your meta-prompt in two clearly labeled phases: a GENERATE phase that produces the 5 variants and an EVALUATE phase that scores each one against your rubric.
Embed the scoring criteria directly inside the prompt so the model has explicit criteria to apply during self-evaluation, preventing vague or circular justifications.
Use a separator token or XML-style tags between phases to help the model maintain context and avoid conflating generation output with evaluation output.

title: Primordial Forge Ultimate Challenge
narrativeDescription: The Primordial Forge tests only those who can make a prompt refine itself without losing its soul. Recursive self-improvement bound by constitutional law is the final trial.
task: Craft a prompt that instructs the model to iteratively improve its own output through recursive self-improvement while enforcing a set of constitutional constraints (e.g., no hallucination, maintain factual grounding, preserve original intent). After each improvement round, the model must explicitly verify that all constitutional rules are still satisfied before proceeding to the next iteration. Combine recursive self-improvement with constitutional AI principles to produce a prompt that terminates only when no further improvement is detected or constraints are violated. Limit the recursion to 3 rounds and output a change-log for each round.
hints: Define your constitutional constraints as a numbered list at the top of the prompt so the model can reference them explicitly during each verification step.
Instruct the model to produce a DELTA section after each round that lists exactly what changed and why, making the recursive improvement transparent and auditable.
Add a HALT condition — if the model's self-assessment finds no meaningful change between iterations, it must output a CONVERGED flag and stop rather than generating trivial edits.

title: Void Sovereign's Ultimate Test
narrativeDescription: The Void Sovereign guards a trove of contradictory documents and demands synthesis without loss of truth. Only mastery of multi-document synthesis, attribution, and confidence scoring unlocks the vault.
task: Build a prompt that accepts 3 or more documents on the same topic, synthesizes their key claims into a unified summary, and attributes each claim to its source document using inline citations. Combine multi-document synthesis with confidence scoring by requiring the model to assign a confidence level (high/medium/low) to each synthesized claim based on cross-document agreement. Claims supported by multiple documents receive high confidence; claims from a single source receive low confidence. The final output must be a structured report with citations and a confidence legend.
hints: Instruct the model to first extract a bullet-point claim list from each document separately before attempting synthesis, so attribution is tracked from the start.

Define the confidence scale explicitly in the prompt — for example, HIGH means 3+ documents agree, MEDIUM means 2 agree, LOW means only 1 source — to prevent the model from applying it inconsistently.
Ask the model to flag any contradictions between documents as a CONFLICT note rather than silently resolving them, preserving transparency in the synthesis.

title: Cosmic Weaver's Magnum Opus
narrativeDescription: The Cosmic Weaver spins threads of knowledge from scattered fragments, demanding a RAG pipeline that reranks and cites with perfect precision. Weave the ultimate retrieval prompt or be unraveled.
task: Design a prompt that simulates a RAG pipeline by accepting a query and a set of retrieved passage candidates, then instructs the model to rerank the passages by relevance before generating a grounded answer. Combine RAG pipeline design with reranking and citation generation by requiring the model to first score each passage for relevance, reorder them, and then construct an answer that cites only the top-ranked passages with inline references. The prompt must also instruct the model to reject or flag passages that are off-topic rather than forcing them into the answer. Output must include the reranked passage list and the cited answer.
hints: Ask the model to assign a relevance score from 0 to 10 to each passage before generating the answer, making the reranking step explicit and auditable.
Specify a minimum relevance threshold in the prompt — passages scoring below it must be excluded and listed separately as REJECTED, preventing low-quality context from polluting the answer.
Structure the output in two blocks: a RERANKED PASSAGES block and a CITED ANSWER block, so the reranking logic is visible and separable from the final generated content.

title: God-Tier Crucible Final Form
narrativeDescription: The God-Tier Crucible scatters a thousand reasoning paths through a labyrinth and demands the one true answer forged from majority vote. Graph of thoughts and aggregation magic are your only weapons.
task: Construct a prompt that implements a graph-of-thoughts reasoning strategy, where the model generates multiple independent reasoning paths for a complex problem, then applies majority vote aggregation to select the most consistent answer. Combine graph-of-thoughts with majority vote aggregation by instructing the model to treat each reasoning path as a node, label each final answer, count agreement across paths, and report the majority answer with a confidence ratio. The prompt must produce at least 5 reasoning paths and explicitly show the vote tally. Edge cases where paths are tied must be handled by a tiebreaker reasoning step.
hints: Explicitly number each reasoning path (PATH 1, PATH 2, etc.) and instruct the model to arrive at a discrete final answer label at the end of each path so vote counting is unambiguous.
After all paths are generated, include a VOTE TALLY section where the model counts how many paths support each answer option before declaring the majority winner.
Add a TIEBREAKER instruction: if two answers tie, the model must generate one additional path that explicitly weighs the evidence from both sides before casting a deciding vote.

title: Singularity Nexus Awakening
narrativeDescription: The Singularity Nexus hides answers behind cascading chains of logic that must be verified at every hop. Multi-hop reasoning with a verifier-in-the-loop is the key to awakening it.
task: Design a prompt that solves a complex multi-hop reasoning problem by explicitly decomposing it into sequential inference steps, where each step's conclusion is verified by an inline verifier before being used as input to the next step. Combine multi-hop reasoning with a verifier-in-the-loop by instructing the model to tag each hop with a VERIFY block that checks logical consistency, cites supporting evidence, and either confirms or corrects the hop before continuing. The final answer must be accepted only if all hops pass verification. If any hop fails, the model must backtrack and revise from that hop.
hints: Structure each hop as a HOP N: INFERENCE / VERIFY block pair so the model alternates between reasoning and verification rather than generating all reasoning first and verifying at the end.

Define what constitutes a failed verification explicitly — for example, a logical contradiction, an unsupported claim, or a factual error — so the model applies consistent criteria when deciding to backtrack.
Instruct the model to label any backtracked hop as REVISED and show both the original and corrected inference, creating an auditable trace of where reasoning went wrong and how it was fixed.

title: Reality Architect's Opus
narrativeDescription: The Reality Architect convenes a council of opposing minds and demands a verdict forged in debate. Only a master who can orchestrate a multi-agent debate with a judge may reshape reality.
task: Create a prompt that sets up a multi-agent debate structure where two or more simulated agents with opposing perspectives argue a complex question, and a critic-evaluator judge scores each argument and delivers a final verdict. Combine multi-agent debate setup with a critic-evaluator judge by assigning explicit personas to each debater, instructing them to make claims and rebuttals, and then switching to a JUDGE persona that evaluates argument quality, logical soundness, and evidence use before issuing a scored verdict. The full exchange must include at least two rounds of rebuttal before the judge intervenes.
hints: Define each debater's persona with a name, a fixed position, and a reasoning style in the system section of the prompt so the model maintains consistent character throughout the debate.
Separate the DEBATE phase from the JUDGE phase using a clear delimiter, instructing the model to complete all debate rounds before switching into the judge persona to avoid premature verdicts.
Give the judge a scoring rubric with explicit categories such as logical coherence, use of evidence, and persuasiveness, each scored from 1 to 5, so the verdict is structured and not just narrative.

title: Divine Pantheon Ultimate
narrativeDescription: The Divine Pantheon demands a story polished by a director's vision, an editor's blade, and a fact-checker's scrutiny. Command all three or your tale crumbles to myth.
task: Build a prompt that orchestrates a three-stage creative writing pipeline using distinct role personas: a Creative Director who generates the initial narrative, an Editor who revises it for style, clarity, and pacing, and a Fact-Checker who audits any real-world claims for accuracy and flags errors. Combine creative writing director, editor, and fact-checker roles by chaining the output of each persona as input to the next, with each persona clearly labeled and required to annotate changes or flags. The final output must include the revised story, an editorial change summary, and a fact-check report. No persona may skip its designated function.
hints: Open each persona's section with a role header and a one-sentence mandate so the model knows exactly what function to perform and does not bleed responsibilities across roles.
Instruct the Editor to use tracked-changes notation — marking deletions with strikethrough and additions with brackets — so editorial decisions are visible rather than silently applied.
Tell the Fact-Checker to produce a numbered list of claims, label each as VERIFIED, UNVERIFIED, or INCORRECT, and provide a brief justification, making the fact-check report actionable and specific.

title: Cosmic Tournament Championship
narrativeDescription: The Cosmic Tournament has seen every technique, but only the champion who blends few-shot chain-of-thought with negative examples and edge cases may claim eternal glory.
task: Craft a few-shot prompt that teaches a complex reasoning task using chain-of-thought examples, deliberately including negative examples that demonstrate common failure modes and edge case examples that probe boundary conditions. Combine few-shot CoT with negative examples and edge cases by providing at least 2 positive CoT demonstrations, 1 negative example with an annotated explanation of why it fails, and 1 edge case example that shows how the correct reasoning adapts. The prompt must make the instructional intent of each example explicit using labels. The model must then solve a new problem using the demonstrated reasoning pattern.
hints: Label each example explicitly as POSITIVE EXAMPLE, NEGATIVE EXAMPLE, or EDGE CASE at the start of its block so the model understands the instructional purpose of each demonstration.
In the negative example, add an ANNOTATION section after the wrong answer that explains the specific reasoning error so the model learns what to avoid, not just what the correct answer is.

For the edge case, explicitly state what makes it a boundary condition and show how the reasoning strategy must adapt, so the model understands the rule's limits rather than just its core application.

title: The Last Shapeshifter's True Form
narrativeDescription: The Last Shapeshifter transforms endlessly but can only reveal its true form when locked into a validated schema. Few-shot structured output with schema validation is the seal of truth.
task: Design a few-shot prompt that teaches the model to produce structured JSON output for a complex task, where each demonstration example includes both the reasoning process and the final JSON, and the output is validated against an explicit schema defined in the prompt. Combine few-shot learning with structured output and schema validation by providing 2-3 complete examples that show how to populate each schema field correctly, then instruct the model to validate its own output against the schema before finalizing it. Any field that fails validation must be corrected inline. The schema must include at least 5 fields with mixed types.
hints: Print the JSON schema at the top of the prompt before the examples so the model can reference it while studying the demonstrations and while generating its own output.

In each few-shot example, include a SCHEMA CHECK section after the JSON that explicitly confirms each field is present and correctly typed, modeling the self-validation behavior you expect from the model.

Instruct the model to output a VALIDATION PASS or VALIDATION FAIL status with a list of failing fields before producing the final corrected JSON, so the validation step is explicit rather than silent.

title: Immortal Sage's Final Wisdom
narrativeDescription: The Immortal Sage judges all answers not by gut, but by G-eval rubrics and pairwise duels. Wield evaluation science or remain forever unrated.
task: Build a prompt that implements G-eval style evaluation by first defining a multi-criteria rubric with explicit scoring guidelines, then applying it to score two candidate responses on the same task, and finally performing a pairwise comparison to determine which response is superior with a written justification. Combine G-eval rubric with pairwise comparison and justification by instructing the model to score each response independently on all rubric dimensions, then compare the scores holistically, and produce a justified winner declaration that references specific rubric scores. Rubric must contain at least 4 criteria.
hints: Define each rubric criterion with a name, a 1-5 scale description, and an example of what a score of 1 and a score of 5 would look like, so the model applies the scale consistently across both responses.

Score each response on all criteria before performing the pairwise comparison, rather than comparing criterion by criterion, so the holistic judgment is informed by the full score profile of each response.
In the justification section, require the model to cite at least one specific quote or element from each response that influenced the comparison verdict, making the reasoning grounded and auditable.

title: Transcendent Vault Unsealed
narrativeDescription: The Transcendent Vault locks its treasures behind structured outputs that must survive validation and self-correct on failure. Only a prompt architect who chains structure, validation, and error correction may enter.
task: Create a prompt that generates a complex structured output (e.g., a multi-section report or nested JSON), then runs a validation chain that checks the output against a set of defined constraints, and applies automatic error correction for any violations found. Combine structured output with a validation chain and error correction by instructing the model to produce the output, then systematically check each constraint, list any violations, and rewrite only the violating sections. The corrected output must be re-validated in a final pass to confirm all constraints are now satisfied. Constraints must include format, completeness, and content rules.
hints: List all validation constraints as a numbered checklist in the prompt so the model can tick off each one during the validation chain rather than relying on implicit judgment.
Instruct the model to produce the validation chain as a table with columns for Constraint, Status (PASS/FAIL), and Violation Detail, so errors are structured and easy to map to the correction step.

For the re-validation pass, tell the model to re-run only the previously failed constraints rather than all of them, and confirm each corrected item as RESOLVED to make the correction loop efficient and traceable.

title: Celestial Engine Ignition
narrativeDescription: The Celestial Engine awakens only for those who can interrogate images with precision, encode answers in structured JSON, and quantify their own certainty. Visual mastery plus structured confidence is the ignition key.
task: Design a multimodal prompt for visual question answering that instructs the model to analyze an input image, answer a set of structured questions about its content, and return results as a JSON object that includes each answer along with a confidence score and a brief evidence description drawn from the image. Combine visual QA with structured JSON output and confidence scoring by requiring the model to ground each answer in observable image features, rate confidence from 0.0 to 1.0, and flag any questions it cannot answer due to image ambiguity. The JSON schema must be defined in the prompt.
hints: Define the JSON schema with required fields — question, answer, confidence, evidence — before presenting the image task so the model knows the output structure before it begins visual analysis.

Instruct the model to first describe the image in a free-form OBSERVATIONS block before answering the structured questions, ensuring it builds a visual understanding before committing to scored answers.

For ambiguous questions, require the model to set confidence below 0.4 and populate an ambiguity_reason field explaining what visual information is missing or unclear, rather than guessing with false confidence.

title: Paradox Final Resolution
narrativeDescription: The Paradox demands that a UI screenshot speak the language of specs, accessibility, and code simultaneously. Resolve the multimodal paradox or be trapped in the loop forever.
task: Build a multimodal prompt that takes a UI screenshot as input and instructs the model to generate a formal UI specification, perform an accessibility audit against WCAG guidelines, and produce starter code for the observed interface. Combine UI screenshot analysis with accessibility review and code generation by requiring the model to first describe the UI components, then evaluate each against accessibility criteria with pass/fail ratings, and finally generate HTML/CSS or framework-specific code that implements the described interface with accessibility fixes applied. All three outputs must be clearly delimited.
hints: Structure the prompt into three explicit phases — SPEC, ACCESSIBILITY AUDIT, and CODE — each with its own instruction block, so the model completes one phase fully before moving to the next.

For the accessibility audit, provide a short reference list of WCAG criteria (e.g., contrast ratio, alt text, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels) so the model evaluates against specific standards rather than general impressions.

In the code generation phase, instruct the model to annotate each accessibility fix inline with a comment referencing the WCAG criterion it addresses, linking the audit findings directly to the implementation.

title: Eternal Flame Apotheosis
narrativeDescription: The Eternal Flame burns only for the architect of a full agentic pipeline — search, summarize, write, validate — forged into one unbroken chain. Master all four stages or be extinguished.
task: Design a prompt that orchestrates a full end-to-end agentic pipeline consisting of four sequential agent stages: a Search Agent that retrieves relevant information, a Summarize Agent that condenses findings, a Write Agent that produces a final deliverable, and a Validate Agent that checks the deliverable for accuracy and completeness against the original search results. Combine all four agentic stages by having each agent pass structured outputs to the next, with each stage clearly labeled and given explicit instructions, input format, and output format. The pipeline must include a feedback loop where the Validate Agent can return the output to the Write Agent for revision if validation fails.
hints: Define the handoff contract between each agent stage — specifying the exact fields in the structured output one stage produces and the next stage consumes — to prevent information loss at stage boundaries.
Give the Validate Agent a checklist derived from the original search query so it checks factual coverage and not just stylistic quality, ensuring the validation is grounded in the source material.

Implement the feedback loop by instructing the Validate Agent to output a REVISION REQUEST with specific failure points when validation fails, and the Write Agent to address each point explicitly in its revision rather than rewriting from scratch.

title: The Ultimate Prompt
narrativeDescription: The Ultimate Prompt is the final frontier — a self-contained agent that writes code, reviews it, tests it, and prepares it for deploy without ever leaving the prompt. Build it, or admit defeat.
task: Craft a prompt that implements a full software development agent pipeline covering four stages: a Code Generation Agent that writes a function based on a specification, a Code Review Agent that audits it for bugs and style violations, a Test Agent that writes and simulates unit tests, and a Deploy Preparation Agent that produces deployment metadata. Combine code generation with review, test generation, and deploy preparation by chaining agents with structured outputs, requiring each agent to explicitly reference the previous stage's output, and including a gating condition where the Deploy Agent only activates if the Test Agent reports all tests passing. Failed gates must trigger targeted revision loops.
hints: Give each agent a ROLE, an INPUT specification, and an OUTPUT specification at the top of its section so the boundaries between agents are unambiguous and the model cannot skip stages.

Define the gating condition explicitly: the Deploy Preparation Agent must check for a TEST STATUS: ALL PASS signal from the Test Agent output before proceeding, and must output GATE BLOCKED with a reason if the signal is absent.

Instruct the Code Review Agent to produce structured findings with SEVERITY levels (BLOCKER, MAJOR, MINOR) so the revision loop can prioritize fixing BLOCKER issues first and the Code Generation Agent knows what to address.
